name: Jamaica

colors.symbolism: the wealth of the country and the beauty of its sunshine
the lush vegetation and agricultural resources of the island, and hope

description: A gold saltire (diagonal cross) divides the flag into four triangles. The triangles at the hoist and fly are green; those at the top and bottom are black.
history: Adopted on 6 August 1962, the day Jamaica gained independence from the United Kingdom. The design was selected by a bipartisan committee of the House of Representatives after a national competition produced no winning entry. The original symbolism stated 'Hardships there are, but the land is green and the sun shineth' — black for the hardships, green for the land, gold for the sun. In 1996 the official interpretation of black was changed to emphasise strength and creativity rather than hardship. Jamaica is the only country whose national flag does not include red, white or blue.

features.symbolism: a gold saltire dividing the flag into four triangles — green at hoist and fly, black at top and bottom
